title: Cestichis alpina
intro: Cestichis alpina, also known as Alpine Cestichis, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Australia and New Zealand. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cestichis alpina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cestichis alpina include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cestichis alpina is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. The genus Cestichis is a small group of terrestrial orchids, and Cestichis alpina is one of the species within this genus. Cestichis alpina is native to the Himalayan region and is often found in alpine meadows and rocky areas. Cestichis alpina is a rare and threatened species due to habitat loss and degradation.
